text: ...to players. And right now, currently, the toughest guy in the Philippines, the man of the week, he proved today why he is.
And it's a cool, you know.
Filipino billiard master Dennis Orkulyo of Surigao is this year's Predator International 10-Ball Champion.
Orkulyo dominated at the Royal Ballroom inside the Riviera Hotel and Casino here in Las Vegas.
During the first round, the 30-year-old billiard master scored right away before he let Rolf Suque of Germany strike the ball.
Ang nangyari kasi, natalo yung kababayan natin, si Alex Pagulayan.
Sabi ko, sana magantihan ko yung kalaban natin para at least, baga tayo ang mananalo.
Pero ganun pa man, hindi rin ako nakakasiguro dahil yung kalaban natin, magaling yun.
Isa sa pinakamagaling sa Germany.
So, sabi ko, gagawin ko na lahat yung magagawa ko na sana swertein ako sa posisyon ng bola.
Suque admits, Filipinos in general are among the best billiard players in the world.
They are great cue artists, so to speak.
They definitely born with a pool stick, it seems like.
Even without knowing that he is a Filipino, you can tell just by the way they stroke the ball.
They have a lot of knowledge about the game and they are definitely one of the pool leading countries in the world.
With a score of 8-3, Orkulyo bags the title for the 2009 Predator International 10-Ball Championship.
Predator International President Karim Belhaj predicts that before the end of this year,
Orkulyo will be the best and the number one billiard player in the world.
This is one of the largest events in the world, one of the greatest events in the U.S. and a Filipino one.
Not only a Filipino one, but you have the three out of the last four, three Filipinos.
Orkulyo heads back to the Philippines with a trophy and $20,000 in his pocket.
The Filipino billiard players coming.
All the way from the Philippines reigned this year's Predator International 10-Ball Championship.
Precision and skills of the Pinoys in holding the cue sticks proved to be the best in the world of billiards.
Bev Lorente, ABS-CBN News, Las Vegas.
